name: Helen Wong

work_experience.title: Management Trainee
work_experience.description: Started career at OCBC as a management trainee. Became OCBC's first China Desk Manager in Hong Kong.

work_experience.title: Various senior roles including CEO Greater China
work_experience.description: Spent 27 years at HSBC, rising to Chief Executive for Greater China. Developed deep expertise in capital markets, syndicated finance, and corporate banking.

work_experience.title: Deputy President and Head of Global Wholesale Banking
work_experience.description: Joined OCBC in February 2020 to head the newly created global wholesale banking division.

work_experience.title: Group Chief Executive Officer
work_experience.description: First woman to lead one of Singapore's 'Big Three' banks. Led OCBC to record profits for three consecutive years through 2024. Retired end of 2025 to spend time with family in Hong Kong.

work_experience.title: Executive Director
work_experience.description: Appointed Executive Director of OCBC board.

person_relationships.description: Tan Teck Long succeeded Helen Wong as Group CEO of OCBC on 1 January 2026. She had been mentoring him during the transition period.
Helen Wong succeeded Samuel Tsien as OCBC Group CEO in April 2021.

major_achievements.achievement: Appointed Group CEO of OCBC, becoming the first woman to lead one of Singapore's 'Big Three' banks.
Led OCBC to record profits for three consecutive years through 2024, outpacing peers with the 'one group' strategy.
Named one of the most powerful women in banking by Fortune.
Drove sustainability initiatives including achieving carbon neutrality for operational emissions and joining the UN-convened Net-Zero Banking Alliance.

biography_summary: Helen Wong Pik Kuen (born 1963, Hong Kong) is a Singaporean banker who served as Group Chief Executive Officer of OCBC Bank from April 2021 to December 2025, making history as the first woman to lead one of Singapore's 'Big Three' banks. She began her banking career in 1984 as a management trainee at OCBC, later becoming the bank's first China Desk Manager. She spent 27 years at HSBC where she rose to become CEO for Greater China, developing deep expertise in the region. She returned to OCBC in February 2020 as Deputy President and Head of Global Wholesale Banking before being appointed Group CEO in April 2021. Under her leadership, OCBC achieved record profits for three consecutive years through 2024 and she was named one of the most powerful women in banking by Fortune. She retired at the end of 2025 and currently serves as Non-executive Chairman of OCBC China and Non-executive Director of OCBC Hong Kong.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with data from Reeracoen, Fortune, OCBC official site, Euromoney, and Grokipedia. Full career timeline, birth date, birth place, biography, achievements, and relationships added.
